Vehicle access at Claydon Junction. We have a documented agreement with the landowner for a reserved right of access off the main road, up the farm track past Shepherds Furze Farm to the Railway Line, however please note the following restrictions: 1. No vehicles exceeding 10 tonnes gross weight. 2. Strict speed limit of 15mph. 3. Access for all staff between 9am - 5pm on Monday - Friday ONLY. 4. Access at weekends will only be allowed following prior arrangement with the landowner. 5. Access for signallers is 24/7. 6. No parking of machinery, plant or materials on land not belonging to Network Rail. 7. Mis-use of this access arrangement may result in the agreement being terminated and access stopped. Anyone using this access outside the documented hours may also be reported to police and could be liable to prosecution for trespass.

Ardley Cutting and Quarry. Inclues 4 miles of railway line and cutting slopes <2 miles on either side of Ardley Station>. Geological interest for its jurassic rocks and interest with limestone grassland, acncient woodland and wet habitats. Notably wide range of vertibrates. Large population of the internationally protected great crested newts. Consult Site management before undertaking work on cuttings. A Site Management Statement has been agreed for this site. Consult English Nature on 01635 268881 or Midlands Environment Manager on 0121 345 3412 prior to working on line.

ELR Name: WHITACRE JCN - PROOF HOUSE VIA WATER ORTON
Free Text: New access compound created as part of HS2 works. Currently this is a site shared between BBV and Network Rail. As you go through the gates from the A47, Heartlands Parkway, the Network Rail compound is on your right and the BBV site, which we don't have access to, is to the left. The gates will have a joint locking ( Daisy Chain) arrangement so that BBV and Network Rail staff can always enter. These locks must always be put back on in the daisy chain arrangement. Once inside the NR area, there is parking and room for the storage of RRV's and materials but all storage of materials and plant should be agreed with the PIC or with the Whitacre P-Way section. There is a High Pressure Gas main through the site and this area has been fenced off. No materials are to be stacked or stored within this area and warning signed are posted. The approach ramp to the RRAP has yellow vehicle barriers on it and these should only be opened when the lines are under possession or a line block, to allow access to the RRAP. There is also a clump of Japanese Knotweed near the gas main which is being treated over the next few years by approved contractors.

Free Text: Mill Lane Crossing Is located at the bottom of a steep track which is also used as a bridleway & footpath.  Care should be taken when driving up & down this road.  The track is presently rough & small car derived vans may bottom out on the track.  Once at the trackside it is possible to drive along the old track bed adjacent to the running lines to 89m 50ch to the North & 87m 70ch to the South.  Care to be taken when parking so as not to obscure sighting of approaching trains to users of the crossing.   LAT:N52:06:08    LONG: W1:19:22

ELR Name: DIDCOT - BEYOND CANNOCK ROAD JN. (DIDCOT & CHESTER LINE)
Free Text: Grid Refs: SP389596 and SP366608 for start and end of SSSI. Harbury railway cutting SSSI. Including most of BR land between 100 MP at station & 101.5M West of Station but excluding permanent way. Some land let to Warwickshire Nature Conservation Trust & Agricultural letting to Mr E H Moore. The cutting occupie 33ha of limestone grassland, scrub and woodland. The site also supports 30 species of butterfly. Keep ditches and culverts clear at all times. A Site Management Statement has been produced for this site. Consult English Nature on 01295 257601 or Midlands Environment Manager on 0121 345 3412 prior to working on line.
